steps.title: Use vertex form
steps.content: A quadratic with vertex $(h,k)$ can be written as $f(t)=a(t-h)^2+k$. The maximum height occurs at $t=2.1$ and is $70.56$, so the model must include $(t-2.1)^2+70.56$.

steps.title: Choose the sign
steps.content: The object rises to a maximum and then falls, so the parabola opens downward. Therefore $a$ must be negative.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content: Only A, $f(t)=-16(t-2.1)^2+70.56$, has the correct vertex and opens downward.
